insight: There's been no measurable rain lately, so there's no runoff driving contamination, and strong summer sunlight is helping disinfect the water. The model's main signals right now: hours since high tide is lowering risk — the timing relative to high tide affects how concentrated bacteria are at the shoreline; strong sunlight is lowering risk by inactivating bacteria with UV; and high tide level is lowering risk — a high tide can mobilize bacteria held in wet sand and push the waterline toward outfalls. The most recent sample, 7 days ago, was excellent at 5 MPN/100mL. It's a naturally clean, well-flushed open-coast beach (unsafe only ~3% of the time), so outside of storms it's reliably safe.
